events.name: Brazil Frost Season (Coffee)
events.why: Brazil produces ~35% of world coffee. June–August frost events in Minas Gerais can destroy crops. Even forecasts (not confirmed events) move futures 10–30% in days.
events.action: Watch Brazil weather daily June–Aug. On first frost warning: LONG coffee ETF (JO) immediately. Hold 5–7 days. Exit on weather normalization. Use 4% stop.

events.name: S&P 500 Index Rebalancing
events.why: S&P additions force index funds (trillions AUM) to buy the new component before the effective date. Announced ~5 trading days ahead. Average impact: +5–8% announcement to effective date.
events.action: Buy addition immediately on S&P announcement (before 4PM). Hold through effective date. Sell on effective date open as passive funds finish buying. Short deletions 1–2 days after.

events.name: Fed Bank Stress Test Results
events.why: All-clear results unlock dividend hikes and buyback authorizations. The bank with the highest capital buffer raises its dividend most aggressively → immediate re-rating.
events.action: 14 days before results: LONG well-capitalized bank basket. After results: rotate into specific banks announcing largest capital returns. Exit 4 weeks post-announcement.
events.exit_trigger: 4 weeks post-results OR any major fail

events.name: Bitcoin Options Expiry (Max Pain)
events.why: Monthly/quarterly BTC options expiry on Deribit moves $5–15B notional. Price gravitates toward max pain strike (where most options expire worthless) in the week before expiry.
events.action: 7 days before major expiry: check max pain on Deribit. If spot > max pain: expect downward pressure. If spot < max pain by 8%+: LONG IBIT. Exit ON expiry day.
events.exit_trigger: Expiry day OR max pain price achieved

events.name: Semiconductor Lead Time Cycle
events.why: Semi lead times are a 6-month leading indicator. Expanding lead times → supply shortage → pricing power → margin expansion. Equipment makers lead the cycle by 6 months.
events.action: When Susquehanna lead time data shows expansion YoY: LONG SOXX and equipment (AMAT/KLAC/ASML). When contracting 3+ months straight: reduce/exit. Full cycle: 12–18 months.

events.name: Hurricane Season (Energy Volatility)
events.why: Gulf of Mexico produces ~17% of US oil. Category 3+ storms disrupt rigs and refineries. NOAA seasonal forecasts in June move energy stocks before storms form.
events.action: LONG USO/UNG when NOAA predicts above-normal season (June). Scale up on Cat 2+ storm formations targeting Gulf. Sell within 48h of landfall.

events.name: Ad-Tech Q4 Revenue Peak
events.why: Retail ad budgets are 'use it or lose it.' CPM rates spike Oct–Dec as brands compete for holiday eyeballs. Revenue and guidance beats cluster in this window.
events.action: Accumulate META/GOOGL/TTD in late September. Hold through Q3 earnings (Oct). Exit before Q4 earnings in January. Avoid holding into post-holiday ad slowdown.
events.exit_trigger: After Q3 earnings beat OR December 31

events.name: Sovereign Wealth Fund Rebalancing
events.why: Norway GPFG ($1.7T), Saudi PIF ($700B), ADIA ($850B) rebalance quarterly. Large equity drawdowns force them to buy to restore allocation. Their flows are disclosed with a 30–90 day lag.
events.action: After market correction >15%: expect SWF buying within 30–60 days. LONG broad market ETFs 3 weeks into the selloff — especially if oil nations are flush (high oil = SWF inflows).
events.exit_trigger: Market recovers 10%+ from entry OR 90 days

events.name: Indian Wedding Season (Gold)
events.why: India consumes 25% of global gold jewelry. Dhanteras festival (Oct/Nov) is the biggest gold-buying day of the year. Consistent seasonal demand pattern for decades.
events.action: LONG GLD/IAU from October 1. Scale up before Dhanteras. Hold through Diwali. Exit mid-December as wedding season peaks.

events.name: January Effect (Small-Caps)
events.why: Tax-loss selling dumps small-caps in December. January sees institutional repositioning. Small-caps historically outperform large-caps in the first two weeks of January.
events.action: LONG IWM/VBR around Dec 26–31 (after tax-loss selling bottoms). Exit January 15. The setup is in December, not January.

events.name: Chinese New Year
events.why: Factories shut 7-10 days. Pre-holiday industrial restocking drives an oil demand surge 2 weeks before. Post-holiday inventory builds often reverse the move sharply.
events.action: Go LONG crude oil ETFs (USO/UCO) 14 days before the holiday. Target +5-8%. Exit 2 days before start. SHORT side post-holiday if EIA inventory data shows builds.
events.exit_trigger: T−2 before holiday start OR −3% stop loss

events.name: M&A Arbitrage (8-K Filings)
events.why: On acquisition announcement, target spikes to ~90% of deal price, leaving a 5–10% spread. The spread reflects deal-close probability. Wider spread = higher regulatory risk.
events.action: On announcement: LONG target at market. Size based on spread width. Monitor antitrust news daily. Exit at deal close OR immediately on deal-break rumor. Max 3% of portfolio.

events.name: IPO Lock-up Expirations
events.why: Insiders locked out 180 days from IPO. Expiry floods market with supply. Stock historically drops 5–15% in the two weeks surrounding expiry.
events.action: SHORT target 14 days before lockup expiry. Cover on/after expiry day. Do NOT short if stock is already down 30%+ from IPO price. Avoid if earnings catalyst is nearby.

events.name: Post-Earnings Drift (PEAD)
events.why: Markets systematically under-react to earnings surprises. Stocks with large positive EPS surprises continue drifting up for 60 days. Academic literature confirms this anomaly persists.
events.action: After large EPS beat (+10% above consensus): LONG next trading day. Hold 30–60 days. Use 20-day ATR as stop. Works best with revenue beat AND guidance raise.

events.name: Share Buyback Announcements
events.why: Buybacks >5% of float generate average +12% over 12 months vs market. Signals management confidence. Reduces share count = EPS accretion.
events.action: LONG on large announcement (>3% float). Add on any pullback in 30 days. Confirm FCF can fund it — avoid debt-funded buybacks in high-rate environments.

events.name: Insider Cluster Buying
events.why: 3+ unique insiders buying in the same 30-day window signals conviction. Cluster buying outperforms market by 8–10% in the following year vs single insider buys.
events.action: On cluster detection: LONG within 48h. Prefer CEO/CFO/Director over VP. Avoid if pending M&A (arbitrage incentive). Hold 6–12 months minimum.

events.name: Gaming Blockbuster Launch
events.why: AAA launches (GTA, COD) drive GPU demand spikes, hardware upgrades, and publisher revenue beats. Pre-order numbers are a leading indicator 90 days out.
events.action: 3 months before: LONG publisher + NVDA. 1 month before: add platform holder (MSFT/SNE). Exit on launch weekend — sell the news.
events.exit_trigger: Launch day OR pre-orders miss expectations

events.name: FDA PDUFA Drug Approval Dates
events.why: PDUFA is FDA deadline for approval/rejection. Average binary move: +40–60% (approval) or −50–70% (rejection). IV collapses after — options decay sharply post-decision.
events.action: Use straddle options 30 days before (buy both calls and puts). The binary move exceeds IV pricing 60% of the time. Size max 2% portfolio. Exit day-of or next morning.

events.name: Copper Price Momentum
events.why: 'Dr. Copper' predicts global industrial demand 3–6 months ahead. Sustained 3-month uptrend correlates with EM equity outperformance and commodity rotation.
events.action: When copper breaks 3-month high with rising 20-day MA: LONG miners (FCX/SCCO). Hold until copper makes 3-month low or miners hit 30% profit. Avoid when USD strengthening sharply.

events.name: Shipping Container Rate Cycle (SCFI)
events.why: Shanghai Containerized Freight Index predicts retail gross margins 2–3 months ahead. Spiking rates → inventory build → margin compression → retailer earnings misses.
events.action: When SCFI rises 50%+ over 8 weeks: SHORT broad retail (XRT). When SCFI normalizes from spike: LONG retailers. Shipping stocks trade WITH rates — opposite direction.
events.trade_type: SHORT retailers on spike / LONG on normalize

events.name: War / Ceasefire Alerts
events.why: Conflict escalation → oil spike + defense surge + gold safe-haven. Ceasefire → oil dump + defense pullback. Mean reversion on ceasefires is fast (1–5 days).
events.action: Escalation: LONG LMT/RTX/GLD within hours. Exit within 5 trading days. Ceasefire: SHORT defense within 24h. Cover in 3 days. Oil only if Middle East or Russia involved.

events.name: Regulatory Fines / Investigations
events.why: Initial regulatory news causes 10–30% selloff. 73% of cases end in settlement, not prosecution. Settlement is often below fear-implied penalty — mean reversion opportunity.
events.action: After >15% single-day drop on regulatory news: LONG if strong balance sheet and no prior fraud. 1–2% max position. Hold through settlement. Avoid if CEO/CFO is the target.

events.name: Analyst Upgrade Cluster
events.why: 3+ upgrades in 30 days from different firms signal an institutional consensus shift. Tier-1 upgrades (GS/MS/JPM) with 15%+ PT raises generate +6% 30-day alpha vs market.
events.action: On 3rd upgrade in 30 days from different firms: LONG same day. Hold 30–45 days. Exit if stock already up 15%+ since first upgrade (alpha already captured).
events.exit_trigger: 45 days OR stock +15% from first upgrade

events.name: AI Compute Capacity Milestones
events.why: Hyperscaler CAPEX guidance and new cluster deployment announcements move the entire AI supply chain. 2026 trend: power infrastructure is the bottleneck — alpha shifts from chips to utilities and networking.
events.action: On large CAPEX announcement: LONG power enablers (VST/CEG) and networking (ANET) before chips (NVDA already priced). Hold 60–90 days. Exit if AI spending narrative reverses.
events.exit_trigger: CAPEX guidance lowered OR AI narrative reverses
